posts.cooked: This is probably a question with an easy answer, but is there a way to tell right off the bat if a Jadot wine is domaine vs negoce when buying online?

posts.name: Brady Daniels
posts.cooked:
I haven’t checked recently, but I believe the front label of Jadot domaine wines are printed with, “Récolté, vinifié, élevé, et mis en boteilles par Louis Jadot,” as well as having the words “Domaine Louis Jadot”

For example some Bonnes Mares have those words. If they aren’t there, I think one should assume it’s the negotiant version. This may have changed in recent years, and if so, sorry for the old info.
Domaine appears on the ones I have as well. Not sure if that’s universal though.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
We got into this quite thoroughly recently: https://www.wineberserkers.com/forum/viewtopic.php?p=2864489#p2864489

The spoiler is that it says it on the label.

posts.cooked: I just went down to the cellar and did a quick check. I have an unopened 6 pack of 2012 CSD which has Domaine Gagey on the wood box. Also a few boxes of Chapelle, also unopened that have domaine on the box. I didn’t open and look at the bottles but sure hope they all say the same! I have some others too but didn’t look around.

For example some Bonnes Mares have those words. If they aren’t there, I think one should assume it’s the negotiant version. This may have changed in recent years, and if so, sorry for the old info.

quoted you both for the same question. is there a good way to tell when shopping on a website? for example: William on the link you posted, the OP didnt even realize they had bought the Negoce bottling because it is really hard to tell from some retailers, who just list the wine as “Louis Jadot Bonnes Mares” (for example).

I’ll link an example at JJBuckley below. the wine is just listed as “Louis Jadot Volnay Clos des Chenes” (and luckily JJbuckley gives a picture which is HOPEFULLY of the bottle in question, but some sites (like benchmark) dont. the second link is NSG Vaucrains, which is listed the same. the picture shows Domaine Louis Jadot for the Chenes but not for the Vaucrains. that is the only way to know? do we have to ask for specification or bottle pics for websites with no picture?

posts.name: Brady Daniels
posts.cooked:
I’m afraid I think your only solution is to ask the retailer.

Agree. But be very specific in your request. Ask whether the bottle or case actually says “Domaine.” Honestly most people don’t know that there are two different BM’s.

posts.name: Gary Fordyce
posts.cooked:
Go to the Jadot website, find your bottle, click on the photo, and it will say whether the bottling is from a Jadot Domaine (e.g., Domaine des Héritiers Louis Jadot, Domaine Gagey, Domaine Louis Jadot).
